Tests the ability to add properties to a Boolean object in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code demonstrates the ability to add properties to a Boolean object in JavaScript. It creates a new Boolean object `b` with an initial value of `true` and then assigns a new property `x` with the value `1` to this object. The `assert` function checks that the property `x` of the Boolean object `b` is equal to `1`, confirming that properties can be dynamically added to Boolean objects. This script serves as a simple test case to illustrate the behavior of Boolean objects in JavaScript.
# Global Variables

---
### b
- **Type**: ``Boolean` object`
- **Description**: The variable `b` is a global variable that is an instance of the `Boolean` object initialized with the value `true`. It has an additional property `x` set to the value `1`.
- **Use**: Stores a `Boolean` object with an additional property `x` for further operations or assertions.
